title: Quantum average correlation based on average coherence
authors: Xiaoyu Ma, Qing-Hua Zhang, Cong Xu
abstract: This paper studies the quantification and structural properties of quantum average correlation based on average coherence. Motivated by two mathematically equivalent approaches to define average coherence: one by averaging over complete sets of mutually unbiased bases, and the other by integrating over all orthogonal bases under the Haar measure, we define an average correlation for bipartite systems as the difference between global and local skew information. This correlation measure is shown to satisfy essential properties including non negativity, contractivity under local quantum channels, and local unitary invariance. We further prove the equivalence between the average correlation defined via mutually unbiased bases and that defined via unitary groups. Finally, we derive a complementarity relation that connects wave-particle duality with the average correlation between a system and its environment.
summary: This paper introduces a new way to measure quantum correlations that does not depend on the choice of basis. By using Wigner-Yanases skew information, the authors show that averaging over different sets of bases (like MUBs or all possible unitaries) yields the same correlation value. They also discover a new rule for quantum systems: the loss of local wave-particle duality is exactly balanced by the correlation between the system and its environment. This provides a deeper understanding of how entanglement and correlations affect local quantum properties.
main_problem: The paper aims to develop a basis-independent (intrinsic) quantum correlation measure for bipartite systems to address the basis-dependent nature of existing measures.
main_result: The authors prove the equivalence of average correlation measures defined via MUBs, unitary group integration, and orthonormal bases, and derive a complementarity relation linking wave-particle duality to system-environment correlations.
method: The study utilizes the Wigner-Yanase skew information framework, applying Haar measure integration over the unitary group and summation over Mutually Unbiased Bases (MUBs).
model_or_system: The study focuses on bipartite quantum systems (rho_AB) and bipartite pure states (rho_AE) within a composite Hilbert space, specifically considering multi-path interferometry contexts.
key_observables: Wigner-Yanase skew information, average coherence, quantum average correlation, wave feature (W), and particle feature (P).
important_parameters: Hilbert space dimensions (dA, dB), Schmidt coefficients (lambda_mu), and the existence of complete sets of MUBs for prime power dimensions.
main_assumptions: The existence of a complete set of Mutually Unbiased Bases (MUBs) is assumed for dimensions that are prime powers.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of basis-dependence, defines average coherence and correlation using skew information, proves the mathematical equivalence of different averaging methods, establishes the properties of the new correlation measure, and concludes with a complementarity relation for wave-particle duality.
why_interesting: It provides a fundamental link between local wave-particle duality and global system-environment correlations, offering a new way to quantify how information is lost to the environment in open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
